title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · Collaboration Transfer
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer rebase, sequencer, autosquash, and topology preservation into a new rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G77 is a lab in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-rebase-equivalence-recovery-71tcfg" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Rebase Equivalence Recovery into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-rebase-equivalence-recovery-71tcfg
# scenario-key: trace-71tcfg probe-gfct17 boundary-tcfg71 owner-71tcfgx fault-71tcfgz
# before-ref: main=a11ce71 candidate=b00b1fg
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce71 candidate=c0ffeeg
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
